title: Chartered Accountant
description: A Chartered Accountant in India performs daily tasks such as auditing financial statements, preparing tax returns, and providing financial consulting services to clients. They also conduct financial analysis, prepare financial reports, and ensure compliance with regulatory requirements. Additionally, they may work with clients to identify areas of financial improvement and develop strategies to achieve their financial goals.
top_govt_colleges: Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI)
Institute of Cost Accountants of India (ICAI)
top_private_colleges: Narsee Monjee Institute of Management Studies (NMIMS)
Birla Institute of Technology and Science (BITS) Pilani

career_progression.year_0_2: Initial years involve clearing CA Foundation and CA Intermediate exams, followed by articleship training under a practicing CA.
career_progression.year_3_5: After clearing CA Final, one can start practicing as a CA, taking up audits, tax consulting, and other financial services.
career_progression.year_6_10: With experience, one can move to senior roles, such as partner in a firm, or start their own practice.
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: A CA can start their own practice, offering services such as auditing, tax consulting, and financial advisory.
career_progression.global_pathway: Chartered Accountants can work abroad in countries like the UK, Australia, and the US, but may need to obtain additional certifications or licenses.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Starting out as a CA can be challenging, with long working hours and intense competition.
common_misconceptions: CA is only for commerce students
